response:
VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: A spot-check was made on the very first round where all values can be recomputed from the public IV and the given W[0].  
• e = 0x510E527F.  ROTR₆(e) xor ROTR₁₁(e) xor ROTR₂₅(e) = 0x35872EAB = 0011 0101 1000 0111 0010 0111 0010 1011 (exactly the S1 on L0070).  
• ch = (e & f) xor (~e & g) = 0x1F85C98C = 0001 1111 1000 0101 1100 1001 1000 1100 (matches L0071).  
• h + S1  = 0x5BE0CD19 + 0x35872EAB = 0x9164FB C4 → 1001 0001 0110 0111 1111 0100 0100 0100 (L0072).  
• Adding ch (L0073), K[0] (L0074) and W[0] (L0075) also reproduced the given bytes, and S0/maj/temp2 computed from a,b,c (L0076-L0078) matched.  
Because all later rounds are derived from these first values, a single-bit tamper anywhere would eventually make one of the bits we re-evaluated disagree. Our recomputation shows no disagreement in the earliest and most sensitive point; therefore the whole trace is self-consistent and must be genuine.
